_comment: Tier boundary numbers for the supervised load orchestrator (docs/ORCHESTRATOR_DESIGN.md #2). Proposed starting defaults, not scientifically derived -- grounded in this project's own documented history where possible (the 2% tier-2 ceiling comes from ROADMAP #14's dogfood run: 5/100 mock Account inserts failed on a known DUPLICATE_VALUE:Jigsaw cause), otherwise chosen by rounding conservative per the design's own governing principle. Git-tracked, human-tunable -- same convention as batch_size_heuristics.json/field_synonyms.json. A human reviews and commits changes deliberately; nothing here is hardcoded in Python.

prod._comment: Materially tighter than uat, per the design's own requirement -- a clean UAT pass at a looser tier doesn't imply the same object should get the loose PROD tier (PROD access is separately gated by the graduation ladder in reference/orchestrator_trust_ladder.json anyway).
